title: Pungent Earthy Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: This Tunisian lamb tagine is a flavorful and aromatic stew made with tender lamb, earthy spices, and a burst of fresh lime juice, all wrapped in a traditional lotus leaf wrap.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper and cook for 1 minute.
Add the katsuramuki-cut lamb shoulder and cook until it is browned on all sides.
Add the freshly squeezed lime juice and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the Dutch oven with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the lamb tagine for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Remove the Dutch oven from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Wrap each serving of the lamb tagine in a large lotus leaf and serve hot.
